summary: Matched a first slice of `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c`, raising the file report to 25.66% matched for an 8.13 KB fighter-common file. The PR replaced several `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`/`UNK_RET` stubs with typed implementations around entry/end, rebirth-wait, capture, damage, and joint-material setup paths, and made supporting type/header fixes in fighter, player, item, baselib, and symbol metadata. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented `ftCo_800C6AFC(HSD_JObj*)` as a small joint-matrix dirtying helper: return on null or already-dirty JObj, otherwise call `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`.

Implemented `ftCo_EntryEnd_Anim` to decrement `fp->mv.co.entry.timer`, optionally call `ftColl_8007B760(gobj, p_ftCommonData->x6C8)` when `Player_GetFlagsBit4(fp->player_id)` is true, then call `ftCommon_8007D92C` when the timer reaches zero.
Implemented `ftCo_800C7070` to change motion state to `ftCo_MS_RebirthWait` and set fighter flags `x2219_b2` and `x2219_b1` true.
Implemented three closely related wrappers `ftCo_800C7220`, `ftCo_800C7294`, and `ftCo_800C7308` with a Kirby branch and nested inline-call chain to preserve matching behavior.
Implemented `ftCo_800C7434` with a special `FTKIND_GKOOPS` path using a separate inline chain that changes to motion state `0xE`; all other kinds use the rebirth-wait inline chain.

Implemented `ftCo_800C74F4` capture-leadead guard logic using `fp->dmg.x1860 == 16`, `fp->capture_timer == 0`, motion-state exclusion, and `it_802EAF28(fp->dmg.x1868_source) == NULL`; it calls `ftCo_800C7590` and returns true only when the guard passes.
Implemented `ftCo_800C7C60(ftCo_GObj*, int)` as a wrapper around `Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C(GET_FIGHTER(gobj), damage_amount)`.
Implemented `ftCo_800C8064` to load a joint from `Fighter_804D6504`, fetch its DObj/MObj, and store the resulting material pointer in `ft_804D6580`.
Updated function prototypes in `ft_0C31.h` from unknown signatures to concrete signatures for `ftCo_800C6AFC`, `ftCo_800C7590`, and `ftCo_800C7C60`.

Renamed fighter fields `x2108` to `capture_timer` and `x210C_walljumpInputTimer` to `wall_jump_input_timer`; propagated the wall-jump timer rename through `ftwalljump.c` and capture-timer use through `fighter.c` and `ft_0C31.c`.
Typed `Fighter_804D6504` as `HSD_Joint*` instead of `UNK_T`, and changed `ftCommonData.x6C8` from `UNK_T` to `int`.
Moved/centralized `struct UnkFloat6_Camera` from `ftcamera.c` into `ft/types.h`, making it available outside the camera implementation file.
Added new item header `src/melee/it/items/itgreatfoxlaser.h` declaring `it_802EAF28(Item_GObj*)`, used by the capture-leadead logic.
Changed `Player_GetFlagsBit4` return/slot types from `u8/s32` to `int/int` in both implementation and header to match new callsites/signature needs.
Added a placeholder `struct HSD_Spline` declaration in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.h`.
Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` local sdata symbols at `0x804D3C78` and `0x804D3C80` from named `ftCo_804D3C78/80` objects to local string labels `@240` and `@241`.
Changed `.editorconfig` `[*.s]` indentation handling from `indent_style = ignore` to `indent_style = unset`.
Added TODO cross-reference comments linking `ftCo_800C6150` and `ftCo_800C7070`.
smart_moves: Used narrow type improvements as matching enablers: concrete `HSD_JObj*`, `HSD_Joint*`, `int`, and named fighter fields made the new code express original behavior without broad unknown placeholders.

Preserved codegen for repeated tiny transition helpers with deliberately nested static inlines (`inlineB0/B1/B2` and `inlineC0/C1/C2`), a common decomp tactic when direct calls or shared helpers do not match the original inlining pattern.

Renamed `capture_timer` and `wall_jump_input_timer` based on observed behavior and immediately propagated those names to initialization, per-frame decrement, wall-jump logic, and capture checks, increasing semantic clarity across systems.
Moved `UnkFloat6_Camera` into a shared type header rather than duplicating or keeping it local, reducing future declaration drift.
Introduced the minimal `itgreatfoxlaser.h` header only for the needed external item query rather than leaving an implicit/unknown declaration in `ft_0C31.c`.
Updated symbol metadata for local string constants, suggesting the match depended not only on C code but also on accurate local sdata classification.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter code, small global/type corrections outside the target file can be necessary before the target functions can be expressed cleanly.

Field names should be promoted from offset-based names once behavior is seen at multiple callsites; here `capture_timer` and `wall_jump_input_timer` became justified by initialization, per-frame update, wall-jump timing, and capture gating.
Inlining scaffolds can be intentionally verbose; nested one-line inlines may be required to reproduce original compiler output for repeated transition paths.
Header hygiene matters for matching: replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` once signatures are inferred, and keep declarations synchronized across `.c` and `.h`.
Shared structs discovered in one subsystem should live in a common header when another file needs the type, as with `UnkFloat6_Camera`.
Accurate common-data field types can unlock clean calls; `p_ftCommonData->x6C8` needed to be an `int` for the collision helper call shown in `ftCo_EntryEnd_Anim`.
Symbol table local labels and data:string annotations are part of decomp evidence; mismatched sdata symbol names can misrepresent string/object layout even if C code compiles.
naming_conventions: Fighter fields promoted from raw offsets used lower_snake_case descriptive names: `capture_timer`, `wall_jump_input_timer`.
Existing function prefixes were preserved: `ftCo_` for common fighter routines, `ftWallJump_` for wall-jump code, `Player_` for player APIs, and `it_` for item APIs.
Global fighter data retained existing `Fighter_804D####` address-based names when semantics were still unclear, but the pointer type was improved (`Fighter_804D6504` as `HSD_Joint*`).
Local symbol table entries for compiler-generated/local strings used `@###` labels rather than invented semantic `ftCo_804D...` names.
Temporary inline helpers used neutral local names (`inlineB0`, `inlineB1`, `inlineB2`, `inlineC0`, `inlineC1`, `inlineC2`), indicating they are matching scaffolds rather than stable domain concepts.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced broad stubs with exact small wrappers where behavior was simple: direct calls to `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`, `Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C`, and `ft_8008521C`-style helpers.
Used pre-decrement/decrement forms (`--fp->mv.co.entry.timer`, `--fp->capture_timer`) matching likely instruction ordering instead of more generic arithmetic.
Used explicit null checks and conditional temporary assignment in `ftCo_800C8064` to obtain matching control flow for DObj/MObj extraction.
Used nested static inline chains to force the compiler to emit the desired call/inlining structure for rebirth and Giga Bowser/GKOOPS transition paths.
Adjusted function signatures away from `UNK_PARAMS` so call ABI, argument types, and return handling could match known callers.
Updated `.sdata` symbol labels and string annotations for local constants, reflecting that matching may require data-symbol corrections alongside source changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` functions still reported at 0% after PR #1141, especially `ftCo_800C6408`, `ftCo_EntryStart_Phys`, `fn_800C7DC4`, and `ftCo_800C61B0`.
Search for subsequent renames or type refinements of `Fighter_804D6504`, `ft_804D6580`, and `ftCommonData.x6C8` to see whether their semantics became known.
Check later item headers for `it_802EAF28` to confirm whether `itgreatfoxlaser.h` remained the right home and whether its return type was refined from `UNK_T`.
Look for future cleanup of the matching-only inline helpers `inlineB*` and `inlineC*` if later compiler-context changes allowed clearer source without losing the match.
Search for later uses of `capture_timer` and `wall_jump_input_timer` to validate these field names across more fighter/capture states.
